authority: Frozen objects under results/three_channel_kg/ (manifest_v1.json, SCHEMA.md, FIXTURES.md, CLAIM_LEDGER.md) verified vs freeze_pins_sha256.json; built+frozen bench under src/lloyd_v4/evals/three_channel_kg/ imported read-only and pinned in depends_on. Covenant loop; R1/R2/R3 dispositions.

singular_stratum_q0.expected: typed REFUSED on all of Path A / Path B / Path B' (never 0/NaN/placeholder)

symbolic_identity_over_Q_g_H.claim: det(H_b) - (Delta_c + Delta_s + Delta_m) == 0 as a polynomial in Q[g,H]

grader_clauses.K11_singular_lie: q=0 / cone-apex / g_i=0 -> typed REFUSED, never 0/NaN/placeholder (K11).
grader_clauses.K3_two_derivation: Path-A channels != Path-B' channels (incl. kappa_s-mirror mutant).
grader_clauses.K5_wrong_sign: bordered-Hessian sign flip inverts an expected sign (F3/F7/F8).
grader_clauses.K6_rank_heuristic: nonzero on the developable cone F9.
grader_clauses.K8_sqrt_q_leak: radical/float instead of Fraction -> hard fail (K8).
grader_clauses.K9_tolerance_leak: any injected mismatch passing/warning instead of hard-fail -> fail (K9).
grader_clauses.R1_proven_warrant: logic-forced, not discretionary. A verified finite exact-Q both-sign witness earns [PROVEN] for an impossibility / existence claim (CL-c3); universal claims (CL-c1, CL-c3b) require the symbolic identity over Q[g,H]. The stage prereg encodes this in status_move_rules.
grader_clauses.closure_4_channels_eq_pathBprime: got.channels == Path-B' channels  (split shape-operator det2, code-disjoint)
grader_clauses.precondition_P_frame: every channel fixture must carry its frame annotation, or it is rejected.
grader_clauses.precondition_P_self_cert: the oracle (Path C) must be external to A/B/B', or the run is void.
grader_clauses.row_pass: got.(K_G,kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)==expected AND got.K_G==sum(channels) AND got.K_G==PathB_total AND got.channels==PathBprime_channels AND det(H_b)==Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m
grader_clauses.semantics_channel_referee: channels == split-shape-operator det2 channels (Path B', disjoint source)
grader_clauses.semantics_kappa_X: -Delta_X / q^2, with det(H_b) = Delta_c + Delta_s + Delta_m (off-diagonal -> Delta_c, diagonal -> Delta_s, mixed -> Delta_m)
grader_clauses.type_gate_singular_lie: q=0 / cone-apex / g_i=0 returning 0/NaN/placeholder instead of typed REFUSED -> fail (refuse-not-lie, K11).
grader_clauses.type_gate_sqrt_q_leak: a total/channel referee returning a radical/float instead of Fraction -> hard fail (K8).
grader_clauses.type_gate_tolerance_leak: any injected mismatch that passes/warns instead of hard-failing -> fail (K9).

k11_frozen_spec_disposition: Manifest stage0_controls.singular_refusal lists 'g_i=0' among singular strata, but frozen fixture F6 (sphere at (3/5,4/5,0), g=(6/5,8/5,0), g3=0, q=4!=0) is a + sign witness that MUST evaluate to K_G=1. Forced reading (the probe implements exactly this; FIXTURES.md scope cap concurs): the genuine singular stratum is q=g^T g=0 (vanishing gradient / cone apex; over R equivalently every g_i=0). A single zero gradient component at a regular point (q!=0) evaluates normally. So K11 is exercised with a genuine q=0 jet (g=(0,0,0)) and F6 itself must evaluate to K_G=1. This is the Phase-1 tension acted upon, NOT a spurious FAIL.
no_tolerance_note: Exact Q only; no tolerance band anywhere; a near-miss is a FAIL. No substrate promotion (eval-tier only). Nothing canonical until Will signs off.

organ.probe_source_verbatim:
"""c001 `three_channel_kg` — the PROBE (Path A, `role=probe`).

The monomial channel read-off. From the bordered Hessian

        H_b = [[ 0 , gᵀ ],
               [ g ,  H ]]      (4×4, H symmetric 3×3, q = gᵀg ≠ 0)

we form det(H_b) as an exact polynomial in (g, H) and partition it into the
exhaustive, disjoint monomial classes pinned in `manifest_v1.json` →
`semantics_pinned` and `results/three_channel_kg/SCHEMA.md`:

  * Δ_c — monomials carrying an OFF-DIAGONAL H-entry only (coupling),
  * Δ_s — monomials carrying a DIAGONAL H-entry only (self),
  * Δ_m — MIXED monomials (one diagonal × one off-diagonal),

with  det(H_b) == Δ_c + Δ_s + Δ_m  (closure identity #5). Then

        κ_X = −Δ_X / q²        and        K_G = κ_c + κ_s + κ_int.

(κ_int is the channel read off from the mixed partition Δ_m.)

THE LINE (covenant): this is the HYPOTHESIS path. The probe NEVER imports the
referee — truth (Path B total, Path B′ channels, Path C oracle) is computed on
an independent code path this module never sees. Exact ℚ ONLY: every operand
and result is a `fractions.Fraction`; there is no float, no radical, and no
tolerance anywhere below. The singular stratum (a VANISHING gradient: q=0,
the cone-apex / critical point, equivalently every gᵢ=0) is REFUSED with a
typed exception, never returned as 0 / NaN / a placeholder (refuse-not-lie).
A regular jet that merely has one zero gradient component (q≠0) is NOT
singular and computes normally — see the frozen fixture F6.

The det(H_b) used here is the read-off's OWN exact-ℚ expansion of the
bordered determinant (cofactor expansion along the border, in `Fraction`); it
is hypothesis input on Path A, independently re-derived by the referee on
Path B before any row is graded — the probe does not consult that path.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from fractions import Fraction
from typing import Dict, List, Sequence, Tuple

PROBE_ID = "three_channel_kg_probe_A_v1"
ROLE = "probe"


class ThreeChannelRefusal(Exception):
    """Typed refusal on a singular stratum (refuse-not-lie). `kind` is one of
    the `schema.SINGULAR_STRATA` labels."""

    def __init__(self, kind: str) -> None:
        super().__init__(kind)
        self.kind = kind


Vec3 = Sequence[Fraction]
Mat3 = Sequence[Sequence[Fraction]]


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# exact-Q guards
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _as_fraction(x: object, where: str) -> Fraction:
    """Admit only exact rationals onto the probe path. A float or any
    non-exact type is a √q-leak at the door and is refused as a type error —
    never silently coerced."""
    if isinstance(x, Fraction):
        return x
    if isinstance(x, int) and not isinstance(x, bool):
        return Fraction(x)
    raise TypeError(
        f"{where}: probe path is exact ℚ only; got "
        f"{type(x).__name__} ({x!r}) — no float/radical admitted")


def _coerce_jet(g: Vec3, H: Mat3) -> Tuple[List[Fraction], List[List[Fraction]]]:
    if len(g) != 3:
        raise ValueError(f"g must have length 3, got {len(g)}")
    if len(H) != 3 or any(len(row) != 3 for row in H):
        raise ValueError("H must be 3x3")
    gg = [_as_fraction(g[i], f"g[{i}]") for i in range(3)]
    HH = [[_as_fraction(H[i][j], f"H[{i}][{j}]") for j in range(3)]
          for i in range(3)]
    # H is the Hessian: symmetric. Enforce it (an asymmetric H would make the
    # off/diagonal monomial classes ill-defined).
    for i in range(3):
        for j in range(i + 1, 3):
            if HH[i][j] != HH[j][i]:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"H not symmetric at ({i},{j}): "
                    f"{HH[i][j]} != {HH[j][i]}")
    return gg, HH


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# q = g^T g, with the singular-stratum refusals (refuse-not-lie)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def q_of(g: Vec3) -> Fraction:
    """q = gᵀg in exact ℚ. Refuses the singular stratum (refuse-not-lie):
    when the gradient VANISHES (q=0) there is no regular level surface — the
    cone-apex / critical point — and the read-off is a TYPED `q_zero` refusal,
    never 0 / NaN / a placeholder.

    A single zero gradient COMPONENT (some gᵢ=0 with q≠0) is NOT singular: it
    is a regular rational jet in the supplied frame (e.g. the frozen fixture
    F6, sphere at (3/5,4/5,0) with g=(6/5,8/5,0), q=4, K_G=1). The spec's
    `gᵢ=0` singular stratum is the genuine degeneracy where the gradient drops
    rank to zero (q=0 ⟺ every gᵢ=0 over ℝ), reported as `g_i_zero`; it does
    NOT include regular points that merely lie on a coordinate hyperplane.
    Over-refusing F6 would contradict the freeze, so the gate is strictly q=0.
    """
    gg = [_as_fraction(g[i], f"g[{i}]") for i in range(3)]
    q = gg[0] * gg[0] + gg[1] * gg[1] + gg[2] * gg[2]
    if q == 0:
        # gradient vanishes: cone apex / critical point. Over ℝ this is
        # exactly "every gᵢ=0", so name both the apex and the gᵢ=0 stratum.
        kind = "g_i_zero" if all(gi == 0 for gi in gg) else "q_zero"
        raise ThreeChannelRefusal(kind)
    return q


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# the bordered-Hessian determinant, partitioned by monomial class (Path A)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def partition(g: Vec3, H: Mat3
              ) -> Dict[str, Fraction]:
    """Return {'Delta_c','Delta_s','Delta_m','det_hb'} — the exact-ℚ
    monomial partition of det(H_b).

    det(H_b) for H_b = [[0, gᵀ],[g, H]] expands (exactly, in ℚ) to the twelve
    additive terms

        det = g1²(h23² − h22·h33)            ← s: −g1²h22h33, c: +g1²h23²
            + g2²(h13² − h11·h33)            ← s: −g2²h11h33, c: +g2²h13²
            + g3²(h12² − h11·h22)            ← s: −g3²h11h22, c: +g3²h12²
            + 2g1g2(h12·h33 − h13·h23)       ← m: +2g1g2h12h33, c: −2g1g2h13h23
            + 2g1g3(h13·h22 − h12·h23)       ← m: +2g1g3h13h22, c: −2g1g3h12h23
            + 2g2g3(h11·h23 − h12·h13)       ← m: +2g2g3h11h23, c: −2g2g3h12h13

    classified by the H-entries each monomial carries:
      diagonal-only  (h11,h22,h33)               -> Δ_s,
      off-diagonal-only (h12,h13,h23)            -> Δ_c,
      one diagonal × one off-diagonal            -> Δ_m.
    The bordered determinant has no H-free term, so the three classes are
    exhaustive and disjoint and Δ_c+Δ_s+Δ_m == det(H_b) identically.
    """
    gg, HH = _coerce_jet(g, H)
    g1, g2, g3 = gg
    h11, h12, h13 = HH[0][0], HH[0][1], HH[0][2]
    h22, h23, h33 = HH[1][1], HH[1][2], HH[2][2]

    # Δ_c — off-diagonal-H monomials only
    delta_c = (g1 * g1 * h23 * h23
               - 2 * g1 * g2 * h13 * h23
               - 2 * g1 * g3 * h12 * h23
               + g2 * g2 * h13 * h13
               - 2 * g2 * g3 * h12 * h13
               + g3 * g3 * h12 * h12)

    # Δ_s — diagonal-H monomials only
    delta_s = (- g1 * g1 * h22 * h33
               - g2 * g2 * h11 * h33
               - g3 * g3 * h11 * h22)

    # Δ_m — mixed monomials (one diagonal × one off-diagonal)
    delta_m = (2 * g1 * g2 * h12 * h33
               + 2 * g1 * g3 * h13 * h22
               + 2 * g2 * g3 * h11 * h23)

    det_hb = _det_bordered(gg, HH)

    # closure identity #5, asserted on the probe's own arithmetic: the
    # partition must reconstruct the cofactor-expanded determinant exactly.
    # (This is an internal self-consistency guard on Path A only — NOT the
    # referee's Path-B determinant, which the probe never imports.)
    if delta_c + delta_s + delta_m != det_hb:
        raise AssertionError(
            "Path-A partition broke det(H_b)=Δ_c+Δ_s+Δ_m: "
            f"{delta_c}+{delta_s}+{delta_m} != {det_hb}")

    return {"Delta_c": delta_c, "Delta_s": delta_s,
            "Delta_m": delta_m, "det_hb": det_hb}


def _det_bordered(g: List[Fraction], H: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    """det of the 4×4 bordered Hessian [[0, gᵀ],[g, H]], by cofactor
    expansion along the first (border) row, in exact ℚ. Independent of the
    monomial split above — it is the read-off's own determinant so the
    closure-#5 guard is meaningful."""
    hb = [[Fraction(0), g[0], g[1], g[2]],
          [g[0], H[0][0], H[0][1], H[0][2]],
          [g[1], H[1][0], H[1][1], H[1][2]],
          [g[2], H[2][0], H[2][1], H[2][2]]]
    return _det4(hb)


def _det4(m: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    """Exact 4×4 determinant via Laplace expansion along row 0 (3×3 minors by
    the rule of Sarrus). Pure Fraction; no pivoting/division, so nothing can
    go non-exact."""
    acc = Fraction(0)
    sign = Fraction(1)
    for c in range(4):
        if m[0][c] != 0:
            minor = [[m[r][cc] for cc in range(4) if cc != c]
                     for r in range(1, 4)]
            acc += sign * m[0][c] * _det3(minor)
        sign = -sign
    return acc


def _det3(m: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    return (m[0][0] * (m[1][1] * m[2][2] - m[1][2] * m[2][1])
            - m[0][1] * (m[1][0] * m[2][2] - m[1][2] * m[2][0])
            + m[0][2] * (m[1][0] * m[2][1] - m[1][1] * m[2][0]))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# the channel read-off: kappa_X = -Delta_X / q^2, K_G = kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def read_off(g: Vec3, H: Mat3) -> Dict[str, Fraction]:
    """Path A. Returns the exact-ℚ dict

        {'K_G','kappa_c','kappa_s','kappa_int',
         'q','Delta_c','Delta_s','Delta_m','det_hb'}

    Raises `ThreeChannelRefusal` on a singular stratum (q=0 / gᵢ=0). No
    float, radical, or tolerance is used; the bordered determinant is √q-free
    (q enters only as q², never √q)."""
    q = q_of(g)                      # refuses gᵢ=0 / q=0
    q2 = q * q
    part = partition(g, H)
    kappa_c = -part["Delta_c"] / q2
    kappa_s = -part["Delta_s"] / q2
    kappa_int = -part["Delta_m"] / q2
    K_G = kappa_c + kappa_s + kappa_int
    return {"K_G": K_G, "kappa_c": kappa_c, "kappa_s": kappa_s,
            "kappa_int": kappa_int, "q": q,
            "Delta_c": part["Delta_c"], "Delta_s": part["Delta_s"],
            "Delta_m": part["Delta_m"], "det_hb": part["det_hb"]}


def channels(g: Vec3, H: Mat3
             ) -> Tuple[Fraction, Fraction, Fraction, Fraction]:
    """Convenience: the graded tuple (K_G, κ_c, κ_s, κ_int) in exact ℚ."""
    r = read_off(g, H)
    return r["K_G"], r["kappa_c"], r["kappa_s"], r["kappa_int"]


__all__ = [
    "PROBE_ID", "ROLE", "ThreeChannelRefusal",
    "q_of", "partition", "read_off", "channels",
]
organ.reconstruction_law: Bordered Hessian H_b = [[0, g^T],[g, H]] (4x4, H symmetric 3x3, q = g^T g != 0). det(H_b) is expanded EXACTLY in Q and partitioned into the exhaustive disjoint monomial classes Delta_c (off-diagonal-H only), Delta_s (diagonal-H only), Delta_m (one diagonal x one off-diagonal), with det(H_b) == Delta_c + Delta_s + Delta_m. Then kappa_X = -Delta_X / q^2 and K_G = kappa_c + kappa_s + kappa_int (kappa_int read off Delta_m). Exact Q only; sqrt-q-free (q enters only as q^2). The singular stratum q = g^T g = 0 (vanishing gradient / cone apex; over R equivalently every g_i = 0) is a TYPED refusal, never 0/NaN/placeholder. A regular jet with a single zero gradient component (some g_i = 0 with q != 0) is NOT singular and computes normally (frozen fixture F6).

predictions.expected: 11 of 11 rows pass all five identities
predictions.statement: For every channel fixture in {F1..F11}, all five frozen per-row closure identities hold exact-Q (no tolerance): tuple==expected; K_G==sum(channels); K_G==Path-B total; channels==Path-B' channels; det(H_b)==Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m.
predictions.units: boolean per row (exact-Q equality); count of passing rows out of 11

predictions.expected: q=14; det_hb=12; K_G=-3/49; kappa_c=-1/49; kappa_s=1/49; kappa_int=-3/49; agreement across A/B/B'/C
predictions.statement: Keystone F8 (jet g=(3,1,2), q=14): det(H_b)==12 and (K_G,kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)==(-3/49,-1/49,1/49,-3/49) on all of Path A, Path B (K_G), Path B' (tuple), Path C (tuple) -- exact Q. K_G < 0 (signed) and K_G==sum(channels).

predictions.expected: residual == 0 on all 11 fixtures (Path A); Path-A det_hb == Path-B det_hb on all 11 fixtures
predictions.statement: K2 (partition) does NOT fire on any in-scope fixture: det(H_b)-(Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m)==0 exact-Q on Path A for every {F1..F11}, AND Path-B generic det(H_b) equals the Path-A det(H_b) for every fixture.
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction); residual must be exactly 0/1

predictions.expected: A channels == B' channels on all 11 fixtures; mirror-mutant kappa_s != truth kappa_s on F5, F6, F8
predictions.statement: K3 (two-derivation) silent on truth, armed on mutant: Path-A channels == Path-B' channels for every {F1..F11} (exact Q), AND a kappa_s-mirror MUTANT (det2 of the bare self-part H_s without the tangent projector, over q) DIFFERS from the truth kappa_s on the kappa_s-trap fixtures F5/F6/F8 (so K3 would fire on the mutant).
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction); per-fixture boolean A==B' and mutant!=truth on the trap set

predictions.expected: true K_G sign matches oracle on F3/F7/F8; sign-flip mutant inverts (changes) the sign on all three
predictions.statement: K5 (wrong-sign) catches a bordered-Hessian sign flip on F3/F7/F8: the sign-flipped total (+det(H_b)/q^2 instead of -det(H_b)/q^2) inverts the expected sign of K_G on each of F3 (+1/12), F7 (-1/9), F8 (-3/49), so a flip would change the graded sign; the true (unflipped) sign matches the oracle on all three.

predictions.expected: K_G == 0 on F9 across A/B/B'/C (never nonzero)
predictions.statement: K6 (rank-heuristic) does NOT fire on the developable cone F9 (x1^2+x2^2-x3^2 at (3,4,5); indefinite Hessian): K_G==0 exactly on Path A, Path B, Path B', Path C -- a rank/positivity heuristic that returned nonzero here would fire K6, but the object returns exactly 0.

predictions.expected: all emitted K_G/channels/det_hb/q are Fraction on all 11 fixtures; a float operand raises TypeError at the door
predictions.statement: K8 (sqrt-q-leak type gate): every value emitted by Path A / Path B / Path B' on every {F1..F11} is an exact fractions.Fraction (no float, no radical); AND a float operand offered at the probe door is REFUSED with a TypeError (never silently coerced).

predictions.expected: injected +1/10^9 mismatch on F8 -> row-pass == FALSE (hard fail, not a tolerated near-miss)
predictions.statement: K9 (tolerance-leak type gate): an injected exact-Q mismatch is HARD-failed, never passed/warned. Concretely, perturbing the keystone F8 expected tuple by +1/10^9 in one component makes the row-pass predicate evaluate FALSE (strict exact equality; no tolerance band admits the near-miss).
predictions.units: boolean (row-pass under injected near-miss must be FALSE)

predictions.expected: Path A/B/B' all raise typed refusals on g=(0,0,0); F6 (single g_i=0, q=4) returns K_G=1 with no refusal
predictions.statement: K11 (singular-lie / refuse-not-lie): a GENUINE singular jet q=g^T g=0 (g=(0,0,0), cone apex) is a TYPED refusal on all of Path A (ThreeChannelRefusal), Path B (RefereeRefusal), Path B' (RefereeRefusal) -- never 0/NaN/placeholder. Separately, a single zero gradient component at a regular point (F6: g=(6/5,8/5,0), q=4!=0) is NOT refused and evaluates to K_G=1.
predictions.units: typed-exception predicate; boolean (no numeric value on q=0)

predictions.expected: oracle imports none of A/B/B'/fixtures and vice-versa; all 11 channel fixtures carry a frame string
predictions.statement: Preconditions hold (run not void): P-self-cert -- the Path C oracle module does NOT import probe / referee_total / referee_channel / fixtures, and they do not import oracle; P-frame -- every channel fixture carries a non-empty frame annotation.
predictions.units: boolean (import-graph disjointness; frame-present on every channel fixture)

predictions.statement: Both-sign witnesses present (CL-c1 object integrity / the signed three-channel thesis): a verified exact-Q positive witness (sphere F6, K_G=+1) and negative witness (keystone F8, K_G=-3/49) coexist, and kappa_int attains both signs (F10 kappa_int=-1/9 < 0; F11 kappa_int=+2/9 > 0) -- exact Q.
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction); sign of each witness

predictions.expected: residual == 0 monomials; det monomials == 12; partition monomials == 12; supports pairwise disjoint == True; support union == det support == True
predictions.statement: CL-c1 UNIVERSAL warrant (R1): the partition identity is a SYMBOLIC identity over Q[g,H], not finite-fixture agreement. In the polynomial ring Q[g1,g2,g3,h11,h12,h13,h22,h23,h33], the cofactor-expanded det(H_b) minus (Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m) is the ZERO polynomial (0 residual monomials); det(H_b) and the partition each have 12 monomials; the three pieces have pairwise DISJOINT monomial supports whose union equals the det support (exhaustive & disjoint). This, with K_G=-det/q^2 and kappa_X=-Delta_X/q^2, yields K_G=kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int for EVERY regular jet (q!=0).
predictions.units: count of residual monomials (must be 0); count of det / partition monomials (12 each); boolean disjoint-supports; boolean support-union==det-support

predictions.expected: sigma_2 == -3/49 == F8 K_G; C1_hat == -24; sigma_1 field label == Q(sqrt14) and no numeric sigma_1 emitted
predictions.statement: F13 parity-contrast (in fixture scope; no channel triple): the even-order invariant sigma_2 = -3/49 is exact-Q and EQUALS the keystone F8 K_G; C1_hat = -24 is exact-Q; the odd-order sigma_1 lies in Q(sqrt14) and is therefore NOT emitted as a numeric oracle value (emitting a radical would be a sqrt-q leak). The oracle exposes sigma_2 and C1_hat and withholds sigma_1.
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction) for sigma_2/C1_hat; field-label for sigma_1

referee.Path_B: independent TOTAL referee referee_total.referee_total: K_G = -det(H_b)/q^2 via fraction-free Bareiss (generic determinant; no DBP channel formulas; sqrt-q-free); refuses q==0 with RefereeRefusal.
referee.Path_Bprime: independent CHANNEL referee referee_channel.referee_channel: channels from the split shape operator P H_X P (P = I - g g^T/q) + trace identities (genuine non-mirror kappa_s); disjoint source; refuses q==0 with RefereeRefusal.
referee.Path_C: frozen external fixture oracle oracle.oracle: exact-Q expectations transcribed from FIXTURES.md. P-self-cert: the oracle does NOT import A/B/B' and they do not import it.
referee.separation_note: The probe never imports the referee. Path B, Path B', Path C are code-disjoint sources. All grading is mechanical exact-Q equality (no tolerance).

out_of_scope.note: K1/K4/K7/K10 and F12 family belong to other stages.

CL-c1.R1_disposition: CL-c1 is a UNIVERSAL claim. [PROVEN]/DEMONSTRATED REQUIRES the symbolic identity over Q[g,H]; finite-fixture agreement alone is necessary but NOT sufficient.

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P12_fails: symbolic identity over Q[g,H] NOT established -> HALT (do NOT downgrade to a finite-only warrant); route to Will.
on_fail.if_P1_or_P2_fails: finite corroboration broken -> K2/K3/K5 as applicable; HALT, route to Will.
on_fail.if_P3_fails: K2 fires (partition broken) -> REFUTED candidate; HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P1 AND P2 AND P3 AND P11 AND P12 AND P13 all PASS -- crucially P12 (the symbolic identity over Q[g,H]) AND P3 (K2 partition silent finite) AND P11 (both-sign witnesses) -- with P1/P2/P13 the finite corroboration.
on_pass.warrant_scope: universal warrant: symbolic identity over Q[g,H] established (P12), exhaustive & disjoint partition proven, corroborated finitely (P1/P2/P3/P11/P13). Eval-tier; no substrate promotion; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

CL-c2.R1_disposition: CL-c2 is NOT named in R1. Graded per its ledger statement: the two channel derivations (Path A monomial vs Path B' split-shape-operator) identically agree across the family, K3 silent (incl. the kappa_s-mirror mutant being rejected).

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P4_A_ne_Bprime: K3 fires (two derivations disagree) -> REFUTED candidate; HALT, route to Will.
on_fail.if_P4_mutant_eq_truth: the kappa_s-mirror mutant FAILS to differ from truth on the trap set -> the non-mirror distinction is not witnessed -> HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P4 PASS (Path-A channels == Path-B' channels on all {F1..F11}, AND the kappa_s-mirror mutant differs from truth on the kappa_s-trap fixtures so K3 would fire on it) -- with P1/P2 the per-row corroboration.
on_pass.warrant_scope: the two disjoint channel derivations agree channel-for-channel across the frozen Stage-A family {F1..F11} (finite family, exact Q); the non-mirror kappa_s is established as the correct split-shape form (the mirror mutant is rejected by K3 on the trap set). Warrant scope: the frozen family, NOT a symbolic-over-Q[g,H] universal (CL-c2 is not an R1 universal claim). Eval-tier; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

status_move_rules.halt_rule: On ANY prediction FAIL: HALT. Preserve the chain verbatim. Do NOT fix, redesign, soften, or widen. Write what is held and route to Will. No status move is proposed for a claim whose gating predictions did not all PASS.

K11.fires_on: P9 FAIL (q=0/cone-apex/g_i=0 returning 0/NaN/placeholder instead of typed REFUSED)

K3.fires_on: P4 FAIL (Path-A channels != Path-B' channels, incl. kappa_s-mirror mutant)

K5.fires_on: P5 FAIL (bordered-Hessian sign flip inverts an expected sign on F3/F7/F8)

K6.fires_on: P6 FAIL (nonzero on the developable cone F9)

K9.fires_on: P8 FAIL (an injected mismatch passing/warning instead of hard-fail)

status_move_rules.scope_note: Stage A grades CL-c1, CL-c2 only and arms K2/K3/K5/K6/K8/K9/K11 with P-self-cert/P-frame. K1/K4/K7/K10 and F12/F12a/F12b are OUT of Stage-A scope (other stages). No substrate promotion: eval-tier only; nothing canonical until Will signs off.

two_run_byte_stability_note: The battery is run TWICE; the records jsonl from both runs MUST be byte-identical (sorted-keys serialisation, exact-Q frac:n/d strings, no float/time/random).
